entries.title: Probe Droid Compromises Echo Base

content.fact: An Imperial probe droid finds Hoth, turning Echo Base from hidden refuge into immediate evacuation crisis.
content.injection: In Imperial Probe Finds Hoth, once the probe droid is identified, Rebel scenes should shift toward evacuation urgency and defensive delay. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.

Respect story-position bounds from Imperial Probe Finds Hoth to Imperial Probe Finds Hoth.

entries.title: Ion Cannon Enables Rebel Evacuation

content.fact: Echo Base relies on the ion cannon to disable Imperial vessels long enough for transports to escape.
content.injection: During the Hoth evacuation, the ion cannon is a critical defensive tool; without timing and coordination, transports are vulnerable.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Battle of Hoth and Evacuation.
Respect story-position bounds from Imperial Probe Finds Hoth to Hoth Evacuated.

entries.title: Hoth Ground Battle Is a Delay Action

content.fact: The Rebel ground defense at Hoth is meant to delay Imperial walkers, not win a conventional battle.
content.injection: At Hoth, Rebel troops and snowspeeders should buy evacuation time rather than expect to defeat the Imperial assault outright.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Battle of Hoth and Evacuation.
Respect story-position bounds from Imperial Probe Finds Hoth to Hoth Evacuated.

entries.title: Vader Prioritizes Luke Over Ordinary Victory

context.label: The Empire Strikes Back fallout path

content.fact: Vader’s pursuit of the Rebels is increasingly shaped by his desire to find Luke.
content.injection: In ESB-era Imperial scenes, Vader’s strategic behavior can become distorted by Luke as an objective, not just Rebel suppression.

entries.title: Ozzel’s Death Sets Vader’s Command Tone

content.fact: Vader executes Admiral Ozzel for failure, reinforcing fear-driven Imperial command culture.
content.injection: After Ozzel fails Vader, Imperial officers around Vader should treat mistakes as potentially fatal. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.

entries.title: Piett Promoted Under Vader Pressure

content.fact: Piett rises under Vader’s command after Ozzel’s death, but his authority remains subject to Vader’s lethal expectations.
content.injection: During and after Hoth, Piett can command the fleet but must operate under Vader’s direct pressure. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.

entries.title: Yoda Training Is Intense but Incomplete

content.fact: Luke receives crucial training from Yoda but abandons it before completion to rescue his friends.
content.injection: During ESB and after Bespin, treat Luke’s Jedi training as partial, emotionally tested, and unfinished. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.

entries.title: Dagobah Cave Vision Tests Luke’s Fear

content.fact: The Dagobah cave confronts Luke with fear, aggression, and a symbolic Vader connection before he understands the truth.
content.injection: Use the cave vision as a warning about Luke’s inner risk, not as public confirmation of the Skywalker family secret.

entries.title: Asteroid Chase Shows Han’s Improvisation

content.fact: The asteroid field escape shows Han’s risky improvisation and the Empire’s willingness to pursue through dangerous terrain.
content.injection: In ESB chase context, write Han as daring and technically clever, not as calmly operating under ideal conditions.

entries.title: Bespin Is Sought as a Safe Haven

content.fact: Han takes the Falcon to Cloud City because Lando is an old contact and Bespin seems outside immediate Imperial control.

content.injection: At Bespin, Luke should be brave and Force-sensitive but outmatched by Vader’s experience and manipulation.

entries.title: Vader’s Paternity Claim Is Private to Luke

context.label: Vader Claims Luke as His Son

content.fact: Vader tells Luke that he is his father, but this is not yet public knowledge and Luke does not fully know how to process it.
content.injection: After Bespin and before wider reveal, treat Vader’s claim as Luke’s private crisis; other characters should not automatically know it.
